$comment: Schema audit performed 2026-05-30. Submission flow has changed since the 2025 draft of this file: Smithery no longer documents a `smithery.yaml` repo file. Current docs (https://smithery.ai/docs/build/publish) describe TWO publishing paths — (1) a public HTTPS endpoint registered at https://smithery.ai/new, and (2) a local-stdio path via an MCPB (.mcpb) bundle uploaded via the `smithery mcp publish` CLI. peek is stdio-only (ADR-0007 — no HTTP listener by design), so the MCPB path is the one to use. This file documents that path. The structured fields below feed both the upload form and the CLI metadata; transcribe them at submission time. Confirm field names against the live docs at submission.
name: peek
description: Bring your real authenticated browser session to AI coding agents. Local-first MCP server backed by a Chrome MV3 extension that records rrweb + console + network into a local SQLite database. No telemetry, no cloud — sessions stay on the user's machine.
author: Cubenest

tools.name: list_recent_sessions
tools.description: List recently recorded browser sessions, newest first.

tools.name: get_session_summary
tools.description: LLM-readable narrative summary of one session: pages visited, click/input counts, navigations, error counts.

tools.name: get_session_console_errors
tools.description: List console error messages recorded in a session, oldest first.

tools.name: get_session_network_errors
tools.description: List failed/notable network requests in a session (status >= statusGte or network error).

tools.name: get_user_action_before_error
tools.description: Show the last N user actions (click/type/navigate) before a console error.

tools.name: generate_playwright_repro
tools.description: Generate a runnable Playwright test from the user actions in a session, optionally limited to a time window.

tools.name: get_dom_snapshot
tools.description: Reconstruct the DOM at a timestamp (or selector subtree within it) and return it as HTML.

tools.name: query_dom_history
tools.description: Timeline of attribute and/or text changes for a selector's node over a session.

tools.name: request_authorization
tools.description: Ask the user to authorize an action via the extension side panel (Level 3 act-with-confirm); returns a one-shot confirmToken. Recorded in ~/.peek/audit.log.

tools.name: execute_action
tools.description: Execute an action in the user's browser. Requires per-origin permission Level 3+; destructive-action blocklist always prompts. Recorded in ~/.peek/audit.log.

TODO_AFTER_CWS: Once the Chrome Web Store listing for the companion extension is live, add the CWS URL to the description body so reviewers can install both halves of peek in one flow.
If Smithery's submission form prompts for a 'companion app store URL', paste the CWS listing URL there.
TODO_VERIFY: An MCPB (.mcpb) bundle for @peekdev/mcp does not exist yet — peek-mcp currently ships as an npm tarball. The bundle has to be produced at submission time (the @anthropic-ai/mcpb tooling, or hand-rolled per the MCPB spec). FLAG TO MAINTAINER: this is a Phase 5 follow-up that has to happen BEFORE the Smithery submission; the other three registries don't need it.

Smithery's 'category' enum may have moved off 'developer-tools' (live form should be cross-checked at submission time). If the closest match has changed (e.g. 'utilities', 'browser-automation'), use that.

If Smithery auto-scans the MCPB for tool metadata it will overwrite the descriptions above; only the bundle's manifest matters for what users see. If it does NOT auto-scan, paste the tools[] array verbatim into the form.
